process_description: Shell-and-tube heat exchanger E-201 cooling phenol-acetone process fluid (180°C, tube side) with cooling tower water (30°C, shell side). 30 days of 5-minute data from March 2025. Known issue: declining heat transfer performance, suspected fouling. Chemical cleaning performed on March 25 but achieved only partial recovery. Two cooling water pump trip events occurred during the period.
user_objective: Diagnose the root cause of declining heat transfer performance in E-201. Determine why the March 25 cleaning only achieved partial recovery. Identify what triggered the fouling and recommend preventive actions.
known_faults: Gradual HTC decline starting approximately mid-March 2025
Chemical cleaning on March 25 restored only ~72% of performance
Two cooling water pump trip events during the month
analysis_constraints: Hot inlet is tightly controlled by upstream distillation — unlikely to be root cause
Cold inlet has known diurnal (±1.5°C) and seasonal variations — must be distinguished from fouling signal
Pump trip events are transient — focus analysis on the persistent HTC decline trend
